title: statice limonium subsp. serotina
intro: Statice limonium subsp. serotina, also known as Sea Lavender, is a perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its small, white or pale pink flowers and silvery-gray foliage. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and soothe skin irritations, which has led to its use in treating conditions such as eczema and acne. The health benefits of Sea Lavender are attributed to its antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which can help to protect against cell damage and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, Sea Lavender is often used as an essential oil or dried herb, and can be found in products such as skincare creams, soaps, and teas.
